cover.title: 3 things that broke running our content team on a fleet of agents
cover.sub: Not the model. Every failure was coordination — a source-of-truth problem. That's the line between a demo and production. Swipe →

pairs.broke: Pipeline silently dropped widget blocks. Raw syntax shipped live.
pairs.fix: A silent drop is worse than a loud error. Fail loud, fail in CI — not in front of a reader.

slides.title: 2. the fleet wrote from a stale truth

pairs.broke: We changed our positioning. The agents kept writing from the old one — in parallel.
pairs.fix: One canonical source of truth, read before writing. Felt this from the wrong side.

pairs.broke: 18 editorial widgets available. The fleet used 0. We blamed taste.
pairs.fix: It was reachability, not taste. If an agent can't find the tool, the tool doesn't exist.

slides.title: the money line
slides.body: A fleet reading a stale source of truth doesn't make one mistake. It makes the same mistake in parallel, everywhere — and you pay for it in review.

data.label: tax you pay rereading to rebuild context

cta.title: the fix was a source of truth agents actually read
cta.sub: Full teardown on the blog. trovex is the canonical-context layer we built so a fleet reads one answer, not five stale ones. Open source, public beta.

source: our own content fleet · trovex reread tax ~60% (my run, our repos)
